articles.title: Rubio heads to Europe to try to garner support for Iran war
articles.snippet: Representatives of the world's wealthiest democracies gather in France today for a G7 meeting, where U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio is expected to try to shore up support among reticent allies for the Trump administration's war on Iran.

articles.title: 'My daughter is under the rubble': Inside Tehran as civilian toll of strikes rises
articles.snippet: One month since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran, Tehran residents tell the BBC their lives have been devastated.

articles.title: Senate Votes to Fund Most of D.H.S. in Bid to End Partial Shutdown
articles.snippet: The bill excludes funding for ICE and the Border Patrol but restores it for federal airport security workers. The House could consider the package on Friday.

articles.title: Standoff With Iran Raises Fresh Doubts About Trump's Freestyle Diplomacy
articles.snippet: A jumble of emissaries — a friend, a family member, a dove and a hawk — on the Iran crisis reflects President Trump's improvisational approach.

articles.title: Iran images appear to show land mines scattered by U.S. forces, a first in years
articles.snippet: The land mines were photographed outside Shiraz, a city located about three miles from one of several nearby Iranian ballistic missile sites.

articles.title: How the Hormuz closure could affect food, medicines and smartphones
articles.snippet: The price of a wide range of goods — from food, to smartphones, to medicines — could be affected by the US-Israel war with Iran.

articles.title: Trump's Signature Is Set to Be Added to America's Currency
articles.snippet: President Trump is poised to be the first sitting president to have his signature appear on the U.S. dollar.

articles.title: Judge temporarily blocks Trump administration's Anthropic ban
articles.snippet: The order briefly stops the government from labeling tech company Anthropic a "supply chain risk," calling that "classic First Amendment retaliation."

articles.title: After a decade as a Yale hospital janitor, she is now a doctor there
articles.snippet: "I still can't believe it," said Shay Taylor-Allen, who landed a residency at Yale New Haven Hospital, where she was born and spent most of her adult life on the cleaning staff.

articles.title: A surprise resignation could open the door for an independent to win a Montana Senate seat
articles.snippet: Two-term GOP Sen. Steve Daines shocked Montana when he announced his retirement. Democrats worry a new independent candidate will split their party's vote.
